source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
From an old model train set there are only identical pieces of track to use. Matthias puts 8 such pieces in a circle (picture on the left). Martin begins his track with 2 pieces as shown in the picture on the right. He also wants to build a closed track and use the smallest number of pieces possible. How many pieces will his track use?
<image1>

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is primarily visual
Question explicitly refers to 'left picture' and 'right picture' for the track configurations
Solving depends on the geometry/shape of the identical track pieces and their spatial arrangement, which is not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and image_manifest shows one available image with high readability metadata
multi_step: The solver must infer the track-piece geometry from the images, not just read text
The task asks for the smallest number of pieces to form a closed track from a given 2-piece start, which implies combining visual constraints with an optimization/minimization step
This is more than direct lookup: it likely requires at least identifying turning behavior of each piece and then determining a feasible closed loop size
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='12', so final output format is unambiguous
A candidate solution can be checked against the visual track geometry and closure condition
asset_registry_record.answer_manifest.answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so core semantics are intact
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scoring is strong overall, with high image dependency handled by an available readable image
numeric answer format gives good verifiability for downstream checking
